self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can improve Engine planning quality by making improvement planning consume typed extraction and adaptation output instead of flattening that stage onto the same base planning input.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP bounded result turns the already-landed extraction-to-adaptation seam into a second bounded Architecture implementation target for improvement-stage chaining.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP case, the Engine improvement plan still used only planningInput even though extraction and adaptation already produced typed output inside processSource().
self_improvement.expected_effect: The Engine can now carry forward earlier stage boundaries into improvement planning without touching proof or integration planning in the same slice.

decision.rationale: The current Engine shape justifies one bounded improvement-stage chaining slice as the next product-owned Architecture implementation target.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
